distilleries.name: Destileria Pacharan Zoco
distilleries.address: Palacio de la Vega del Pozo s/n, 31263 Dicastillo, Navarra, Spain
distilleries.distillation_method: Maceration of 100 percent Navarrese sloe berries in natural anisette, no added colourings or flavourings
distilleries.tasting_program: Guided visit through the palace estate and sloe fields, a pacharan workshop and a cocktail-making session in the Guard's House
distilleries.description: Historic pacharan distillery in a neo-Gothic palace near Estella, with sloe fields, a maceration workshop and a make-your-own cocktail finale.

verified.address_quoted: Palacio de la Vega del Pozo s/n, 31263 Dicastillo, Navarra

distilleries.name: Destilerias Acha
distilleries.distillation_method: Triple-distilled spirit with hand-picked Sierra de Orduna sloes, anise redistilled in 130-year-old copper pot stills
distilleries.tasting_program: Visit to the Manuel Acha Liqueur Museum tracing nearly two centuries of distilling on the distillery's first floor
distilleries.description: Founded 1831, one of the Basque Country's oldest distillers; its Amurrio site houses the first Basque liqueur museum alongside pacharan and vermouth production.

distilleries.name: Destilerias La Navarra
distilleries.address: Avenida Serapio Huici 22, 31610 Villava, Navarra, Spain
distilleries.distillation_method: Traditional Navarrese sloe maceration in anise spirit
distilleries.tasting_program: Guided visits to the distillery by request
distilleries.description: Navarra distiller behind the Etxeko and Las Endrinas pacharan brands, offering guided visits by request at its Villava distillery.

distilleries.name: El Colmado de Ezcaray (Aguardiente de Orujo)
distilleries.distillation_method: Slow pot-still distillation of grape pomace, keeping only the heart of the run
distilleries.tasting_program: Artisan shop selling homemade orujo and liqueurs; not a public distillery cellar
distilleries.description: Family Ezcaray shop selling its own slowly pot-distilled aguardiente de orujo and apple liqueur; retail outlet rather than a tour-open cellar.
distilleries.tip: A shop and producer, not a visitable distillery; buy the orujo over the counter.
